summary: I read both guides (`guides/backend-guide.md`, `guides/ui-style-guide.md`) and checked the plan-phase work under review — the technical design (`design-writer/output/design.md`) and the executable specs the spec-writer wrote (`test/lib/pipelines/status_summary_test.rb`, `test/services/pipelines/broadcast_status_test.rb`, `test/integration/pipeline_live_status_test.rb`) — grounding my findings against the actual codebase (`StatusHelper`, `StepRuns::BroadcastCard`, `StepRuns::Claim`). The work is strongly guide-aligned: the derivation is a side-effect-free PORO in `app/lib` using `.for` (correctly not the service `.call`), the broadcast is a thin service mirroring `BroadcastCard` and targeting the smallest DOM unit after commit, controllers stay thin with a persistence-only `with_board` scope, and views reuse the centralized semantic tones, Tailwind Card/type scale, `aria-live="polite"`, and dot-plus-word so status is never color-alone. I found no blocker or major deviations, so I emitted a `needs_work` verdict carrying two minor, non-blocking reconciliation findings: (F1) the summary's `:muted` tone for an aborted/canceled pipeline conflicts with `StatusHelper::STATUS_TONES` mapping `aborted => :danger`, risking two colors for one state against the ui-style-guide's "semantic and reserved" / "one source of truth" rules; and (F2) the board flow uses integration request specs rather than the system tests the backend-guide names for critical flows (justified by the missing system-test base class). The verdict is written to `verdict.json`.
